Patch Set 2:

Hello Reviewers,
After abandoning the previous commits for IP Assign & Naming Service custom resource resolution capability from me and Brinda, I have now created this consolidated commit and review request.

Please review the changes and let me know your commits.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Looks okay to me for Guilin, but I'd like someone else on the dev team to review and +1 it before I merge

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

In order for ResolutionSummary to work, we need to add a couple properties to the resource assignment object, in the same way its done for RestResourceResolutionProcessor. ResolutionSummary exposes key-identifiers which should contain input key mapping. It also exposes generated payload, in the case of restcalls. 

Examples: 
RestResourceResolutionProcessor:81
RestResourceResolutionProcessor:88

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> In order for ResolutionSummary to work, we need to add a couple properties to the resource assignment object, in the same way its done for RestResourceResolutionProcessor. ResolutionSummary exposes key-identifiers which should contain input key mapping. It also exposes generated payload, in the case of restcalls. 
> 
> Examples: 
> RestResourceResolutionProcessor:81
> RestResourceResolutionProcessor:88


If you look at the IpAssignResolutionCapabilityTest.kt, I am able to get the resolution summary, using the below lines of code. So do you still think a change is needed?

val resolvedContent = ResourceAssignmentUtils.generateResolutionSummaryData(resourceAssignments.values.toList(),
resourceDefinitions())
	
log.info(resolvedContent.asJsonType().toPrettyString())

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> > Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > (1 comment)
> > 
> > In order for ResolutionSummary to work, we need to add a couple properties to the resource assignment object, in the same way its done for RestResourceResolutionProcessor. ResolutionSummary exposes key-identifiers which should contain input key mapping. It also exposes generated payload, in the case of restcalls. 
> > 
> > Examples: 
> > RestResourceResolutionProcessor:81
> > RestResourceResolutionProcessor:88
> 
> 
> If you look at the IpAssignResolutionCapabilityTest.kt, I am able to get the resolution summary, using the below lines of code. So do you still think a change is needed?
> 
> val resolvedContent = ResourceAssignmentUtils.generateResolutionSummaryData(resourceAssignments.values.toList(),
> resourceDefinitions())
> 	
> log.info(resolvedContent.asJsonType().toPrettyString())

Yes because this only shows that you are getting the ResolutionSummary object (with empty values). You could add assertions to this test to see if ResolutionSummary object contains your input key mappings and resolved payload - that would ensure correct behaviour.
